phases.name: Error Handling Consistency Across Tier-2 Workflows

items.description: Fix adw_document.ts catch block: commentFinalStatus is referenced (line 249) but never declared — runWorkflow() takes no issueNumber and never calls createFinalStatusComment(). This is a latent ReferenceError crash on any exception.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'issueNumber' adws/workflows/adw_document.ts -- expect match (parameter added)
RUN: grep -q 'createFinalStatusComment' adws/workflows/adw_document.ts -- expect match (properly initialized)
RUN: bun run adws/workflows/adw_document.ts --adw-id test123 2>&1 | grep -v 'ReferenceError' -- expect no ReferenceError

items.description: Standardize catch blocks across all Tier-2 workflows (adw_test.ts, adw_review.ts, adw_document.ts, adw_patch.ts, adw_plan.ts) to use identical shape: writeWorkflowStatus() + commentStep(truncated error) + commentFinalStatus() + return false
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -A5 'catch (e)' adws/workflows/adw_test.ts adws/workflows/adw_review.ts adws/workflows/adw_document.ts adws/workflows/adw_patch.ts adws/workflows/adw_plan.ts -- expect identical structure

items.description: Add --issue CLI parameter to adw_document.ts so GitHub commenting infrastructure (createCommentStep, createFinalStatusComment) is not dead code. Accept issueNumber in runWorkflow() and wire it through.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep 'runWorkflow' adws/workflows/adw_document.ts | grep -q 'issueNumber' -- expect match

items.description: Change adw_patch.ts to read issueNumber from --issue CLI arg instead of process.env.ADW_ISSUE_NUMBER (line 113), matching every other workflow's CLI parsing convention

Replace the hand-rolled patch plan path extraction regex in adw_review.ts (lines 231-241) with a call to extractPlanPath() from utils.ts, which already handles the same absolute/relative/adwId-fallback logic
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -c 'absMatch.*\.md' adws/workflows/adw_review.ts -- expect 0 (inline regex removed)

items.description: Remove the dynamic re-import of exec inside adw_patch.ts runWorkflow() body (line 249: const { exec } = await import('../src/utils')) — exec is already available or should be statically imported at the top of the file
steps_to_verify: RUN: head -50 adws/workflows/adw_patch.ts | grep -q 'exec' -- expect match (static import)

items.description: Replace the 9 structurally identical step functions (runPlanStep, runBuildStep, runReviewStep, runTestStep, runDocumentStep, runResearchCodebaseStep, runProduceReadmeStep, runUpdatePrimeStep, runPatchPlanStep) in agent-sdk.ts with a single generic runSkillStep(skill, args, opts) that builds the prompt string and delegates to createSDK() + consumeQuery(). Keep the existing function names as thin wrappers if needed for type safety.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -c 'createSDK' adws/src/agent-sdk.ts -- expect <= 3 (factory + generic runner, not 9 copies)

items.description: Fix adw_review.ts allStepUsages items to include the ok field ({step, ok, usage} instead of {step, usage}), removing the implicit ok:true mapping at finalization (line 331) that masks actual step failures
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -A1 'allStepUsages.push' adws/workflows/adw_review.ts | grep -q 'ok:' -- expect match on every push

items.description: Update /create-adw and /port-adw templates to document the two-tier pattern explicitly: when to use runStep() (simple sequential) vs taggedLogger() + direct step calls (retry loops, parallelism, state management)
Add guidance for quickPrompt() and ADWState persistence patterns to /create-adw and /port-adw templates, as these are used in 4+ workflows but absent from the templates
